core_mechanism: symmetry-constrained coarse-graining (dimensional-reduction / effective-theory construction) in which short-scale degrees of freedom are integrated out to build a reduced-dimension effective model that exactly preserves a specific discrete symmetry of the full system, thereby reproducing its continuous (second-order) critical transition
brief_reason: The abstract builds a dimensionally reduced effective theory that respects all symmetries of the underlying theory — in particular the center (discrete) symmetry — via perturbative matching, and shows this preserved symmetry yields a second-order phase transition in analogy with the full theory.
